q:
Maria's English wasn't very good. Now it is better. (improve)
 Her English ___.

My bag was here, but it isn't here any more. (disappear)
 My bag ___.

Lisa can't walk and her leg is in plaster. (break)
 Lisa ___ her leg.

Last week the bus fare was £1.80. Now it is £2. (go up)
 The bus fare ___.
a: The bus fare has gone up.

q:
Dan didn't have a beard before. Now he has a beard. (grow)
 Dan ___ a beard.

It was raining ten minutes ago. It isn't raining now. (stop)
 It ___ raining.

I washed my sweater, and now it's too small for me. (shrink)
 My sweater ___.
Hello! I've just ___ to the shops. I've bought lots of things. (been / gone)
a: Hello! I've just been to the shops. I've bought lots of things.

q: Tom has just ___ out. He'll be back in about an hour. (been / gone)
a: Tom has just gone out. He'll be back in about an hour.

q: Alice isn't here at the moment. I don't know where she's ___. (been / gone)
a: Alice isn't here at the moment. I don't know where she's gone.

q: You're very late. Where have you ___? (been / gone)
a: You're very late. Where have you been?

q: I can't find my bag. ___ (you / see / it) anywhere?
I can't log on to the website. ___ (I / forget) my password.
I sent Joe an email this morning, but ___ (he / not / reply).
Is the meeting still going on, or ___ (it / finish)?
The ___ (weather / change). It's colder now.
a: weather has changed. It's colder now.

q: ___ (you / not / sign) the form. Could you sign it now, please?
a: You haven't signed the form. Could you sign it now, please?

q: Are your friends still here, or ___ (they / go) home?
Paul doesn't know what he's going to do. ___ (he / not / decide / yet).
Do you know where Julia is? Yes, ___ (I / just / see / her).
When is David going away? ___ (he / already / go).
Has ___ (your course / start / yet)?

Joe goes out. Five minutes later, the phone rings. (go out)
 I'm afraid Joe ___.
a: I'm afraid Joe has just gone out.

q:
The waiter starts to take your plate away, but you haven't finished. (not / finish)
 Wait a minute! I ___.
a: Wait a minute! I haven't finished yet.

q:
Your friend asks, 'Shall I phone to reserve a table?' (already / do it)
 No, I ___.

Lisa is looking for a place to live. Ask her if she found one. (find / yet)
 Have ___?
a: Have you found a place to live yet?

q:
A friend asks, 'Where are you going for your holiday?' (not / decide / yet)
 I ___.
Is Laura still out? No, she ___ (come back / just).
a: No, she has just come back.
